name: agent-sdk
context: Build autonomous Claude agents that understand codebases, edit files, and run commands via the Agent SDK
info: The Claude Agent SDK (@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk) is a TypeScript library for building autonomous agents powered by Claude. Agents can understand codebases, edit files, execute shell commands, and complete complex multi-step workflows. The SDK provides a programmatic interface to Claude's agentic capabilities — the same capabilities that power Claude Code. Key concepts: creating agent sessions, streaming responses, providing tools and permissions, and handling the agent lifecycle. The SDK requires Node.js 18+ and an Anthropic API key. It builds on the [[anthropic/messages]] API and [[anthropic/tool-use]] but abstracts away the agentic loop, tool execution, and conversation management. For lower-level control, use the Messages API directly with tool definitions.
triggers: build an autonomous agent with claude
embed claude code in my app

sections.title: Installation and setup
sections.body: Install via `npm install @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk`. Requires Node.js 18+. Set `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` environment variable. Create an agent with `new ClaudeAgent({ model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6' })`. The SDK was previously called `claude-code-sdk` — migration guide available in the docs.

sections.title: Agent lifecycle
sections.body: Create an agent with configuration (model, tools, permissions). Send messages with `.message()` which returns an async iterable stream. The agent maintains conversation context across messages. Each message triggers an agentic loop: Claude reasons, calls tools, processes results, and continues until the task is complete or max turns are reached.

sections.title: Tool permissions
sections.body: Configure which operations auto-approve and which require human approval. Bash commands can be whitelisted by command prefix. File writes can be scoped by glob pattern. File reads can be auto-approved globally. This provides security guardrails while allowing autonomous operation for safe operations.

sections.title: Custom tools
sections.body: Extend the agent with custom tools using the same format as [[anthropic/tool-use]]: name, description, input_schema, and an async handler function. The handler receives the parsed input and returns an output object. Custom tools participate in the same agentic loop as built-in tools.
